title: Troubleshooting, Security, Recovery, and Operations Handoff · Linux Operations Board
summary: Operate an unfamiliar simulated Linux host across filesystem, streams, identity, processes, packages, storage, services, networking, automation, security, and recovery without workshop prompts. Planned artifact: Produce a cumulative evidence packet across unfamiliar cases, performance tasks, explanations, and root-cause repairs. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish terminal, shell, command, process, kernel, and operating-system responsibilities in an observed interaction.
Parse command names, options, operands, quoting, and exit status before predicting behavior.
Use man pages, help, type, command -V, info, and project documentation to establish command authority and provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

exam case — A exam Linux incident crosses filesystem, permission, process, service, and network evidence. Preserve a baseline, change one bounded layer, record exit status, and verify rollback on case 3.

A polished result passes one familiar case but fails a boundary case. What is the defensible release decision?

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Operations Handoff symptom.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Linux Kernel Userspace API Guide — Controls stable userspace interfaces and the boundary between command-line tools and kernel behavior.

stimulus.title: Operations Handoff failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A list of commands is a sufficient operations handoff.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Write an accessible runbook with prerequisites, exact observations, safe actions, expected signals, exceptions, recovery, and ownership.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Stop release, localize the cause, repair the smallest responsible layer, and rerun the full changed-case set.
Ignore the boundary result because the primary demonstration passed.
